prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(identify_impacted_shipment origin_segment_1)
(reserve_carrier_for_shipment origin_segment_1 carrier_resource_slot_1)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment origin_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(confirm_transport_mode_for_shipment origin_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(assign_stakeholder_contact_to_shipment origin_segment_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(evaluate_alternate_port_for_segment origin_segment_1 alternate_port_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(confirm_segment_with_stakeholder_contact origin_segment_1 alternate_port_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(release_stakeholder_contact_from_shipment origin_segment_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(release_transport_mode_option origin_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(identify_impacted_shipment destination_segment_1)
(reserve_carrier_for_shipment destination_segment_1 carrier_resource_slot_2)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment destination_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(confirm_transport_mode_for_shipment destination_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(assign_stakeholder_contact_to_shipment destination_segment_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(evaluate_alternate_route_for_destination_segment destination_segment_1 alternate_route_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(confirm_destination_segment_with_stakeholder_contact destination_segment_1 alternate_route_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(synthesize_and_lock_routing_solution origin_segment_1 destination_segment_1 alternate_port_1 alternate_route_1 routing_solution_1)
(release_stakeholder_contact_from_shipment destination_segment_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(release_transport_mode_option destination_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment origin_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(schedule_routing_solution_execution routing_solution_1 origin_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(confirm_origin_segment_service_restored origin_segment_1 routing_solution_1)
(confirm_destination_segment_service_restored destination_segment_1 routing_solution_1)
(release_transport_mode_option origin_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment origin_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(create_claim_for_shipment origin_segment_1 claim_file_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(reapply_recovered_routing_to_segment origin_segment_1 carrier_resource_slot_1 claim_file_1)
(release_transport_mode_option origin_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment destination_segment_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(create_claim_for_shipment destination_segment_1 claim_file_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(reapply_recovered_routing_to_destination_segment destination_segment_1 carrier_resource_slot_2 claim_file_1)
(identify_impacted_shipment recovery_case_1)
(reserve_carrier_for_shipment recovery_case_1 carrier_resource_slot_3)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment recovery_case_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(confirm_transport_mode_for_shipment recovery_case_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(assign_external_approval_to_case recovery_case_1 external_approval_1)
(assign_stakeholder_contact_to_shipment recovery_case_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(apply_incident_report_to_case recovery_case_1 incident_report_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(engage_contact_for_case recovery_case_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(confirm_contact_with_external_approval recovery_case_1 external_approval_1)
(finalize_contact_confirmation_and_handoff recovery_case_1)
(release_stakeholder_contact_from_shipment recovery_case_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(release_external_approval_from_case recovery_case_1 external_approval_1)
(release_transport_mode_option recovery_case_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(identify_impacted_shipment recovery_case_2)
(reserve_carrier_for_shipment recovery_case_2 carrier_resource_slot_1)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment recovery_case_2 feeder_service_option_1)
(confirm_transport_mode_for_shipment recovery_case_2 feeder_service_option_1)
(assign_container_unit_to_case_for_solution recovery_case_2 container_unit_2 routing_solution_1)
(finalize_container_allocation_for_case recovery_case_2 container_unit_2 routing_solution_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(release_transport_mode_option recovery_case_2 feeder_service_option_1)
(assign_equipment_asset_to_case recovery_case_2 equipment_asset_1)
(assign_stakeholder_contact_to_shipment recovery_case_2 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1)
(reserve_equipment_and_mark_case_ready recovery_case_2 equipment_asset_1 carrier_stakeholder_contact_1 container_unit_2 routing_solution_1)
(assign_external_approval_to_case recovery_case_2 external_approval_1)
(finalize_case_approval recovery_case_2 external_approval_1 container_unit_2 routing_solution_1)
(finalize_case_and_create_operational_handoff recovery_case_2)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment recovery_case_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(create_claim_for_shipment recovery_case_1 claim_file_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(reapply_recovered_routing_to_case recovery_case_1 carrier_resource_slot_3 claim_file_1)
(release_transport_mode_option recovery_case_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(reserve_transport_mode_option_for_shipment recovery_case_2 feeder_service_option_1)
(create_claim_for_shipment recovery_case_2 claim_file_1 feeder_service_option_1)
(reapply_recovered_routing_to_case recovery_case_2 carrier_resource_slot_1 claim_file_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many objects and actions. The goal is to achieve (recovery_routing_applied origin_segment_1), (recovery_routing_applied destination_segment_1), (recovery_routing_applied recovery_case_1), and (recovery_routing_applied recovery_case_2).


## Understanding problem scope  
- The problem involves managing multiple objects and actions, indicating complexity.  
- The goal focuses on applying recovery routing to origin_segment_1, destination_segment_1, recovery_case_1, and recovery_case_2.  

## Defining recovery routing goals  
- Recovery routing must be applied to both the origin and destination segments.  
- It also needs to handle two distinct recovery cases, ensuring comprehensive coverage.

## Examining initial state  
- The initial state lacks recovery_initiated for any objects, with no shipments identified or carriers assigned.  
- Objects like origin_segment_1 and destination_segment_1 are affected_shipments, along with recovery_case_1 and recovery_case_2, all part of the shipment hierarchy.  

## Identifying recovery steps  
- To achieve recovery_routing_applied, first need service_restoration_confirmed for segments and cases, followed by create_claim and reapply_recovered_routing.  
- Service_restoration_confirmed for segments requires them to be ready, validated, and have a selected, scheduled routing solution.  

## Exploring case relationships  
- Recovery cases include the segments but are treated separately, both as affected_shipments.  
- Actions like finalize_case_and_create_operational_handoff or execute_handover_to_operations are needed for case confirmation.  

## Analyzing action dependencies  
- Reapply_recovered_routing_to_segment needs case_claim_opened, which requires prior service_restoration_confirmed.  
- Shipment_assigned_to_carrier is necessary for reapply, but no carriers are assigned initially, suggesting an early assignment step.  

## Planning next moves  
- Start with identify_impacted_shipment for each affected_shipment, as it requires no prior identification or routing.  
- This step sets the stage for reserving carriers and progressing through the recovery process.

## Mapping recovery prerequisites  
- Origin readiness and validation require confirming with a stakeholder contact, needing recovery_initiated, assigned contact, and validated port.  
- Destination readiness follows a similar process, requiring recovery_initiated, validated route, and stakeholder confirmation.

## Sequencing resource use  
- Only one transport mode option is available, requiring reserving for origin, completing tasks, then releasing for destination use.  
- Shared resources like contacts and carriers must be managed sequentially, potentially releasing and reassigning as needed.

## Analyzing scheduling specifics  
- Scheduling routing solutions is specific to origin segments, requiring transport mode reserved for origin confirmation.  
- No equivalent scheduling action exists for destination segments, limiting parallel processing.

## Examining case-specific actions
- Cases can use an incident report path for simpler service restoration, like apply_incident_report_to_case, requiring an initiated case and available incident report.
- This path involves engaging contacts and external approvals, leading to finalize_contact_confirmation_and_handoff for service_restoration_confirmed case.

## Addressing resource constraints
- Shared resources like transport modes and incident reports limit parallel processing, requiring sequential reservation and release.
- For case_2, the incident report isn’t initially available, blocking the incident report path without a way to make it available.

## Exploring alternative strategies
- Consider processing segments with full rerouting while using simpler paths for cases, leveraging case_includes_routing_solution.
- Focus on achieving recovery_routing_applied by ensuring each case and segment has service_restoration_confirmed, potentially reusing released resources like claims.

## Optimizing resource allocation  
- Managing transport mode (tmo) involves reserving for origin, completing tasks, then releasing for destination use, ensuring sequential handling with only one tmo available.  
- Carriers and contacts can be assigned, used, and released between origin and destination, allowing reuse without additional resources like containers or permits.

## Simplifying validation processes  
- Validation for origin and destination can follow a stakeholder confirmation path, using evaluate_alternate_port and evaluate_alternate_route_for_destination_segment, without requiring logistics documents.  
- These paths set port_validated and route_validated, enabling readiness and validation without additional documentation, streamlining the process.

## Managing routing and scheduling  
- Synthesizing and locking routing solutions requires both origin and destination to be ready and validated, with no document registration needed for this basic variant.  
- Scheduling routing execution for origin needs the transport mode reserved at that time, allowing for release afterward, while confirmation steps do not require ongoing reservation.

## Handling claim creation  
- Creating claims for service-restored origin and destination requires reserving the transport mode again, ensuring availability during the claim process.  
- The reapply_recovered_routing_to_segment action maintains carrier and claim assignments, allowing reuse without removing prior assignments, simplifying resource management.

## Managing resource sequencing  
- For case_2, finalizing approval doesn’t create a bundle, allowing operational handoff with service_restoration_confirmed.  
- Case_1 follows an incident path, setting service_restoration_confirmed directly via handoff without needing approval first.

## Planning resource allocation  
- Creating claims for segments and cases requires reserving the transport mode (tmo) for each, ensuring availability during the process.  
- Reapplying recovered routing maintains carrier assignments, making carriers available again without removing prior assignments, facilitating sequential processing.  

## Executing segment workflows  
- Processing origin_segment_1 involves reserving a carrier, transport mode, and contact, then validating with an alternate port to get ready.  
- Steps include identifying the shipment, reserving resources, confirming transport, and evaluating alternate ports to achieve readiness and validation.

## Continuing segment workflows  
- Reapplying recovered routing to origin_segment_1 sets recovery_applied, making carrier_resource_slot_1 and claim_file_1 available again.  
- For destination_segment_1, create a claim and reapply recovered routing, ensuring resources like carrier_resource_slot_2 and claim_file_1 are available post-process.  

## Managing resource releases  
- Release the transport mode option for origin_segment_1 after processing, then reserve it for destination_segment_1 to support claim creation.  
- After handling origin_segment_1, release resources like the transport mode to free them up for subsequent steps.  

## Initiating case workflows  
- For case_1, identify the impacted shipment and reserve carrier_resource_slot_3, ensuring transport mode and external approval are assigned.  
- Apply an incident report to case_1, engage contacts, and confirm with external approval, finalizing with a handoff to set service_confirmed.  

## Preparing for case transitions  
- Release stakeholder contact and external approval from case_1 to make them available for case_2, keeping carrier assigned.  
- Release the transport mode option for case_1, ensuring resources like feeder_service_option_1 are available for reuse in case_2.

## Reviewing case workflows  
- Case_1 used carrier_slot_3, initially available, and was reapplied during processing.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
